title: Expense & Travel Policy
description: Business expense standards, air/rail travel entitlements, approval thresholds, and meal caps.

articles.title: Expense Approval Thresholds & General Principles
articles.content:
General Principles:
• Claims must be submitted in the Expense system within 30 days of incurring the cost. Claims past 90 days require Finance Director approval and may be treated as taxable income.
• Itemised original receipts are required for all expenses over GBP 25 (card statements are insufficient).
• Claims must never be split into smaller amounts to circumvent approval limits.
Approval Thresholds:
• Up to GBP 500: Line Manager approval.
• GBP 500 - GBP 2,500: Line Manager + Department Head approval.
• Above GBP 2,500: Department Head + Finance Business Partner approval.
• Client Entertainment > GBP 500/head: Department Head + Compliance (Gifts & Hospitality Register).

articles.title: Air Travel, Rail, Hotel, and Ground Transport Policy
articles.content:
Travel Booking: All travel must be booked through the Travel Management Company (TMC) portal.
Flight Entitlements:
• Flights under 6 hours: Economy Class.
• Flights 6 - 9 hours: Premium Economy Class.
• Flights over 9 hours: Business Class.
• Managing Director (MD) and above: Business Class for any flight duration.
Rail & Ground Transport: Standard Class rail is default (First Class allowed if journey exceeds 3 hours or for confidential work with manager approval). Taxis/ride-hailing reimbursable outside public transport hours or with heavy client materials.
Hotel Accommodation: Booked via TMC portal subject to city nightly rate caps.
Mileage Rates: GBP 0.45 per mile for the first 10,000 miles in a tax year; GBP 0.25 per mile thereafter. Commuting is not reimbursable.

articles.title: Daily Meal Caps, Client Entertainment, and Non-Reimbursable Items
articles.content:
Daily Meal Caps (per person, inclusive of tax/service, exclusive of alcohol):
• Breakfast: Domestic GBP 15 | International GBP 20
• Lunch: Domestic GBP 20 | International GBP 30
• Dinner: Domestic GBP 40 | International GBP 60
Client Entertainment: Must have genuine business purpose. Claims must log names, organisations of attendees, and meeting purpose.
Non-Reimbursable Items:
• Mini-bar, in-room movies, personal entertainment.
• Traffic fines, parking fines, penalty fares.
• Travel insurance for personal trip extensions.
• Spa, gym, or wellness billed to hotel room.
• Unapproved First Class flights or family member expenses.
Corporate Card: Must be used exclusively for business expenses. Statements reconciled monthly in Expense system.

articles.title: Expense & Travel FAQs
articles.content:
Expense & Travel FAQs:
• Lost receipt: A signed Missing Receipt Declaration can be used for claims under GBP 25. Above GBP 25 requires approver discretion with Finance sign-off.
• Working lunch with colleague: Internal working meals are NOT reimbursable except during approved offsite events.
• Delayed flight extra costs: Reasonable additional costs directly caused by flight delays are reimbursable; keep documentation.
• Loyalty points: Personal loyalty points from business travel may be retained, provided booking choices do not increase cost to the Bank.
